title: The Sociology of Emotions in Latin America
abstract: This article addresses the development of the sociology of emotions in Latin America between 2000 and 2019, as a recent field of knowledge in the process of institutionalization. The research focused on six thematic areas - diverse and heterogeneous - during the period: (a) social change, sociability and emotions; (b) social movements and feelings; (c) gender, generations, affectivity, and care; (d) migration and emotions; (e) work, affectivity, and emotions; (f) theoretical reflections and analytical proposals. Critical evaluation of the field reveals three analytical tensions that hinder its consolidation: in the disciplinary boundary-setting (sociology; sociology and anthropology of emotions; philosophy and sociology); in the perspectives of analysis (sociological; sociocultural; philosophical); in the object of research (emotions; emotions and body; body and emotions), with implications for social research.
